description: Create the new Story-to-Video system module (ltx_pro/story_to_video.py) - a dedicated YouTube-series-focused story-to-video pipeline with episode/series awareness, character persistence, and production package export.
steps: Create ltx_pro/story_to_video.py implementing the StoryToVideo class with these methods: __init__(config), load_story(script, title), extract_characters(), set_series_characters(characters), generate_episode_prompts(episode_number), set_visual_style(style, quality), generate_all_videos(scenes), export_production_package(output_dir).

Implement episode/series awareness: StoryToVideo should maintain a _series_characters dict that persists across episodes. Add _episode_history list to track what happened in previous episodes. Include generate_recap_scene() method for 'Previously on...' scenes.

Implement YouTube-optimized features: generate_chapter_markers(scenes) that outputs YouTube chapter marker format (00:00 Title), extract_thumbnail_candidates(scenes) that picks the best thumbnail frame per scene, set_aspect_ratio(ratio) supporting '16:9' (landscape), '9:16' (shorts), '1:1' (square).

Implement cliffhanger detection: _detect_cliffhanger(scene_text) that looks for tension/suspense indicators in the final scene and applies special camera treatment (slow dolly-in, dramatic lighting keywords).

Implement export_production_package(): creates a directory with ordered video clips list, timeline JSON + EDL, character reference sheets (JSON profiles), prompt log, continuity notes between scenes, and Sora-style JSON export for the full episode.

Add _generate_character_consistency_prompt(character_profile) that builds a detailed prompt prefix from the structured character profile, and _generate_scene_transition(prev_scene, next_scene) for smooth transitions.
Add __all__ export list and comprehensive docstrings to all public methods. Guard heavy imports (torch, etc.) so the file passes py_compile.
acceptance_criteria: ltx_pro/story_to_video.py exists with StoryToVideo class implementing all specified methods
YouTube features: chapter markers, thumbnail extraction, aspect ratio support
Cliffhanger detection with special camera treatment
Production package export with all specified outputs
verification: python3 -c "import ast; tree = ast.parse(open('ltx_pro/story_to_video.py').read()); classes = [n for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.ClassDef)]; assert any(c.name == 'StoryToVideo' for c in classes), 'StoryToVideo class not found'"
